title: MacOS Volume Mapping
body:
### Issue Description

on MacOS, `podman run --rm -v .:/app IMAGE` maps `.` as `/` and exposes the entire host filesystem to the container's directory

### Steps to reproduce the issue

run a container image with `.` as current host directory

### Describe the results you received

host filesystem mapped to the container

### Describe the results you expected

solely current working directory 

### podman info output

```yaml
.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
You've supplied a period to a parameter which accepts a number of different inputs, including named volumes.  A period is a wildcard for more than one of those.

To be clear that you intend the local directory you should use `./`

@jorhett docker behaviour on this is to take cwd, I'm not saying it should be 1:1 but as it's something which you don't notice right away until you access or interact with the container it can expose host to riskful commands

This is working properly on Linux

```
$ mkdir /tmp/dan
$ touch /tmp/dan/walsh
$ cd /tmp/dan
$ podman run -v .:/mnt:Z fedora ls /mnt
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/home/dwalsh/.cache/containers/short-name-aliases.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 718a00fe3212 done   | 
Copying config 368a084ba1 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
walsh
```

What error are you seeing on MAC

I think we default to mounting $HOME:/$HOME on the Mac, are you not in the $HOME directory?

```shell
$ mkdir /tmp/test
$ cd /tmp/test
$ pwd
/tmp/test
$ podman run --rm -v .:/mnt fedora ls /mnt
Users
bin
boot
dev
etc
.....
```
it mounts the host root (output truncated)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
